Agree. The Lesser Palatine Nerve (SLN), being closely related to the area operated upon laterally along the skull base, provides critical innervation for both nasal cavity and soft palate areas crucial for proper swallow mechanism functioning independently of laryngeal control. Therefore, the absence of vocal changes combined with aspiration points strongly toward injury here.
